name: Florian
bio: Full-Stack Developer focused on building real products with clean, scalable architecture. Always exploring AI, automation, and the edge of what is possible.
career_objective: Seeking software engineering and AI engineering internships where I can contribute to real products, focus on clean architecture, and continue growing through practical execution.

projects.name: Ava AI Assistant
projects.description: AI copilot that represents Florian professionally to recruiters and collaborators.

projects.name: Blackjack Online Game
projects.description: Browser-based blackjack game with smooth animations, ambient audio, and streak tracking.

projects.name: NoteLink
projects.description: Class-organized academic resource platform with search, pagination, and structured browsing by school.
highlights: Built a class-organized academic resource platform with structured browsing and search.
Implemented a server-validated upload workflow using Supabase quarantine/public storage and uploader-level ownership.
Added a malware-scanning pipeline using VirusTotal before public file release.

projects.name: BuddyBot 2.0
projects.description: Buddybot 2.0 is a mineflayer-based controller that lets you summon an AI-assisted Minecraft helper directly from server chat commands. It listens to latest.log, spawns a bot with context-aware behaviors, and answers player questions using OpenAI models.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

responsibilities: Evaluated multimodal AI outputs across text, image, audio, and video tasks for prompt adherence and quality.
Annotated and reviewed data for transcription, image labeling, and side-by-side model evaluation.
Created synthetic chat interactions and reverse-engineered prompts to support AI training workflows.
Supported daily IT operations, handling service tickets, data audits, and process documentation.
experience.reason_for_leaving: Pursue higher education, software engineering, AI development and career growth opportunities.

responsibilities: Wrote internal documentation to help standardize workflows and improve team consistency.
experience.reason_for_leaving: Promoted from intern to IT Operations Specialist; no departure occurred.

responsibilities: Managed high-volume customer inquiries, ensuring timely resolution and satisfaction.
experience.reason_for_leaving: Transitioned to an IT Operations Intern role at Infotect to better align with my long-term career path in tech.

organizations.name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers (SHPE) - USF Chapter
highlights: Contributed to Hackabull planning and execution through judging logistics, workshops, web support, and sponsor coordination.
Worked closely with the Tech VP on operations and organizational planning.
Helped recruit 20+ judges from companies including Apple, Meta, Amazon, Microsoft, and Bank of America.

leadership_experience.name: Hackabull
leadership_experience.role: Tech Team Contributor / Judging Operations Support
highlights: Helped recruit 20+ judges, including software engineers and professionals from Apple, Meta, Amazon, Microsoft, and Bank of America.
Helped bring Linda, a USF alum and LinkedIn creator, as an in-person judge.
Authored the judging rubric and helped define judging procedures and documentation.

leadership_style.summary: Leadership through empowerment, structure, and inclusion.
principles: Give people ownership and choice over what they work on.
Create clear structure and expectations without becoming rigid.
Support members' growth, learning, and career development.
Preserve team spirit while improving organization and accountability.

highlights: Centered candidacy on structure, inclusion, and sustainable team growth.
Used Hackabull and Tech Team execution work as concrete leadership examples.

preferences: Practical execution and continuous technical growth

notes.scope: Primary context for resume-aligned recruiter conversations.
